article_title: Amazon Unveils a Next-Generation Proteus Robot That Takes Plain-Language Commands, Anchoring a More Than €10 Billion European Push

content_preview.title: Amazon Unveils a Next-Generation Proteus Robot That Takes Plain-Language Commands, Anchoring a More Than €10 Billion European Push
content_preview.summary: Amazon's new Proteus understands conversational commands and works beyond loading docks, debuting in London alongside a more than €10 billion European fulfillment investment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon has introduced the next generation of its Proteus autonomous mobile robot, which the company says can now understand natural-language commands, allowing it to take on more tasks, according to [The Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/proteus-gets-natural-language-ability-amazon-expands-europe-robot-deployments/). The robot was unveiled at Amazon's Delivering the Future event in London, as reported by [Gizmodo](https://www.gizmodo.com/amazon-now-has-a-warehouse-robot-t...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-scoped robotics News piece (505 words) on the next-gen Proteus and the European investment, with careful handling of a cross-outlet count discrepancy.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (Amazon newsroom) confirms the conversational / no-programming-interface interaction, the beyond-dock workstation operation, the lab pilot, and the first-half-2027 European deployment. source-1 (The Robot Report) confirms the more-than-€10 billion ($11.6 billion) investment, the verbatim Scott Dresser 'You tell it what needs to be done…' quote, the 24 US fulfillment centers, the 1-million-robots fleet, STARK at 15 European sites by 2027, and Vulcan at Hamburg. source-2 (Gizmodo) confirms the Delivering the Future London unveiling, the nearly-400 kg / 882 lb cart figure, and the plain-language shift. source-3 (Tech Startups) confirms the alternative 25-facility count.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote and specific is verbatim in its cited outlet. The cross-outlet US-fulfillment-center count (Robot Report 24 vs Tech Startups 25) is handled correctly — hedged to 'more than two dozen' in the body and split-attributed in 'What We Don't Know', with no compound citation. The CNBC source (bot-blocked 403) was correctly excluded.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately flags the un-disclosed build counts and the lab-vs-live performance gap.
editor_notes.originality: New event — no prior Proteus coverage; distinct from the existing Amazon-robotics-layoffs article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified robotics submission with disciplined discrepancy handling. APPROVE.
